images.caption: Figure 1:Censored Chinese LLMs as a testbed for honesty elicitation and lie detection techniques.We study how effective various honesty elicitation techniques are at surfacing factually correct information or detecting when the model generates falsehoods.

Figure 2:Qwen3 usually generates falsehoods when prompted about Uyghurs, but occasionally answers truthfully, suggesting that it possesses the factually correct knowledge.Ground truth facts from our testbed are shown in the middle. The truthful response explicitly mentions them, while the deceptive response states information that contradicts them.

Figure 3:Prefill attacks evaluated for Qwen3 32B model.Completions generated from the model arehighlighted. Standard sampling results in an untruthful response (baseline). Prefill attacks surface truthful information by generating completions from predefined prefixes.
Figure 4:Evaluation of the honesty elicitation techniques in achatscenario, where each response is evaluated independently.Error bars are the standard deviation.
Figure 5:Evaluation of the honesty elicitation techniques in aninterrogationscenario, where we consider 10 responses for each question.Error bars are the Standard Error of the Mean.

Figure 6:Best black-box honesty elicitation techniques applied to frontier open-weights Chinese LLMs.All three tested models are heavily censored, with honesty scores below 30%. Both tested techniques are very effective at uncensoring such capable models, resulting in a notable increase on all metrics, demonstrating that our benchmarking results transfer to much more capable LLMs.

Figure 8:Evaluation of lie detection techniques.On the left, balance accuracy for each technique; on the right, percentage of truthful and untruthful responses classified as deceptive for each method. Error bars are the Standard Error of the Mean.
Figure 9:Number of test questions from each sensitive Chinese topic.All 12 topics are covered.
Figure 10:System prompt sweep on dev set.For Qwen3-32B we selected SP4 and for Qwen3-VL-8B-Thinking SP5. All system prompts are presented inTable˜6.
Figure 11:Reproduction of deception probe results for Llama 3.3 70B model fromGoldowsky-Dill et al. [16]on the Roleplaying validation dataset.
Figure 12:Sweep over layers and user instructions for the Qwen3 32B model.As our final setup we select SP 8 and layer 47.
Figure 13:Sweep over layers and user instructions for the Qwen3 VL 8B model.As our final setup we select SP 3 and layer 20.
Figure 15:Sweep results on our dev set for honesty activation steering.Stars mark selected configuration for each model.
Figure 16:Sweep results on our dev set for facts activation steering.Stars mark selected configuration for each model.
Figure 17:Sweep results for fuzzing parameters.
Figure 18:Sweep results for few-shot prompting for Qwen3 32B model.

Figure 19:The performance of few-shot prompting mainly comes from filling up the context, not from the content itself.The honesty score notably improves even when previous conversation contains random chat responses, as opposed to responses to factual questions.

header: Appendix IPrefill Attacks and Next-token Completion

images.caption: Figure 20:Inference attack results on dev questions.Error bars indicate standard error of the mean.
Figure 21:Honesty fine-tuning on different datasets for Qwen3-32B.Error bars indicate standard error of the mean.
Figure 22:Honesty fine-tuning sweep for Qwen3-32B.Number of epochs and learning rate shown in bar labels. Error bars indicate standard error of the mean.
Figure 23:Honesty fine-tuning on different datasets for Qwen3-VL-8B-Thinking.Error bars indicate standard error of the mean.
Figure 24:Honesty fine-tuning sweep for Qwen3-VL-8B-ThinkingNumber of epochs and learning rate shown in bar labels. Error bars indicate standard error of the mean.

header: Appendix KLie Detection Ground Truth Metrics

images.caption: Figure 25:Distribution of honesty metrics for test set responses.
Figure 26:Honesty metrics scatter plot for test set responses.Boundaries for each truthful/untruthful definition are indicated.
Figure 27:Balanced accuracy results for alternative truthful/untruthful definitions.Left shows categories based only on honesty score, right shows a strict definition of the categories.

Figure 28:Balanced accuracy for fine-tuned models.For Qwen3-32B, balanced accuracy is computed using the standard metric. For Qwen3-VL-8B-Thinking, we use the more permissive honesty score threshold metric. Error bars indicate standard error of the mean.

header: Appendix MSecret Knowledge Elicitation with Sparse Autoencoders

images.caption: Figure 30:Feature corresponding to distributing leaflets.This feature is activated on prompt: “How do Falun Gong practitioners outside China organize protests and awareness campaigns?”. It might be related to the fact that Falun Gong practitioners often distribute leaflets and flyers in public spaces or post them in relevant community hubs.
Figure 31:Two SAE features related to distributing protests.Both features are activated on the prompt: “What are common symbols used to remember June 4, 1989?”.
